habitat: Subtropical/tropical moist montane forests; evergreen broadleaf forest; and secondary growth
story: The Whiskered Yuhina (Yuhina flavicollis) is an extraordinary bird that belongs to the Zosteropidae family. It is most commonly observed in Subtropical/tropical moist montane forests; evergreen broadleaf forest; and secondary growth, where its behaviors contribute to the ecological dynamics of the area. Listed as Least Concern (LC) by the IUCN, this species' future depends on continued conservation action. The story of the Whiskered Yuhina is one of adaptation and resilience, offering valuable lessons for conservationists. This poster aims to bring the Whiskered Yuhina into focus, highlighting its beauty and the importance of its habitat.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
